cot_prefix: Okay, let me see. The question is asking how long the Montgomery Bus Boycott lasted. Let me read through the provided text again to find the dates mentioned. The boycott started on the day Parks went to court, which would be December 1, 1955, since that's when Rosa Parks was arrested. The text says, "The day was a success..." meaning the first day of the boycott was December 1. Then it goes on to say that the community decided to continue the boycott. The boycott ended on December 21, 1956, when the Supreme Court ruled the laws unfair giving equal rights to the community. So from December 1, 1955, to December 21, 1956, that's just over a year. The answer options are (A) Less than a year, (B) Less than a month, (C) More than a year, or (D) More than a month. Since the boycott lasted from December 1955 to December 1956, that's exactly one year.
